_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The TRACE-DM regulation (draft HG published 8 May 2026) addresses the internal Romanian municipal waste supply chain: it governs the point at which private individuals (citizens/households) deliver recyclable waste to **authorized economic operators** — i.e., licensed waste collectors and collection points. The workspace, as a freight forwarder, does not sit within the primary regulated segment (citizen-to-collector transactions). However, the workspace may intersect with this regulatory framework in two ways:

1. **As a transport operator for collected recyclable waste flows**: If the workspace or road subcontractors within Romania transport consolidated recyclable waste from authorized collection operators to sorting or recycling facilities, those upstream operators will be newly subject to TRACE-DM registration. While the transport leg itself is not directly regulated by TRACE-DM, the traceability record attached to any waste shipment will now carry electronic documentation originating in TRACE-DM. Road transport documents (waybills, waste transport annexes under HG 856/2002 and OUG 92/2021) must remain consistent with TRACE-DM transaction records. Legal confirmation is required as to whether the workspace's carriers qualify as 'authorized operators' under OUG 92/2021 Article 19(4), which could create direct TRACE-DM connectivity obligations.

2. **As a logistics partner for humanitarian or film/event cargo involving scrap metal or recyclable materials**: The intersection with OUG 31/2011 (prohibition on acquisition of ferrous/non-ferrous metals from private individuals without the prescribed purchase receipt) is directly amplified by TRACE-DM, which will generate these receipts electronically. Any workspace activity touching scrap metal cargo in Romania must ensure upstream authorization chain compliance.

The National Environmental Guard (*Garda Națională de Mediu*) is the enforcement body. Sanctions are applied under OUG 92/2021 Article 62(1)(a) in connection with Article 48 record-keeping obligations. The Ministry of Environment will publish an annual TRACE-DM operational report to the Government.

*Source: MMAP Public Consultation Notice — Draft HG on TRACE-DM, Romanian Ministry of Environment, Waters and Forests (MMAP), 8 May 2026. https://mmediu.ro/en/transparenta/proiecte-acte-normative/ministerul-mediului-apelor-si-padurilor-mmap-supune-consultarii-publice-proiectul-de-hotarare-privind-instituirea-sistemului-electronic-de-trasabilitate-a-fluxurilor-de-deseuri-municipale-reciclabi/*
